Name: Tangela
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It tangles any moving thing with its vines. Their subtle shaking is ticklish if you get ensnared.

Name: Tangrowth
Effects: Heal 40 damage from each of your [G] Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its vines grow so profusely that, in the warm season, you can’t even see its eyes.

Name: Hoppip
Flavor-Text: It drifts on winds. It is said that when Hoppip gather in fields and mountains, spring is on the way.

Name: Skiploom
Flavor-Text: It spreads its petals to absorb sunlight. It also floats in the air to get closer to the sun.

Name: Jumpluff
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with his or her Active Pokémon.
This attack does 20 damage times the number of your remaining Prize cards.
Flavor-Text: Even in the fiercest wind, it can control its fluff to make its way to any place in the world it wants.

Name: Yanma
Effects: Your opponent reveals his or her hand.
Flavor-Text: If it flaps its wings really fast, it can generate shock waves that will shatter windows in the area.

Name: Yanmega
Ability-Effect: If you have exactly 4 cards in your hand, ignore all Energy in the attack cost of each of this Pokémon’s attacks.
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This six-legged Pokémon is easily capable of transporting an adult in flight. The wings on its tail help it stay balanced.

Name: Yanmega BREAK
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness, Resistance, or any other effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
BREAK-Rule: Yanmega BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

Name: Seedot
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, if this Pokémon would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent’s next turn, it is not Knocked Out and its remaining HP becomes 10 instead.
Flavor-Text: When it dangles from a tree branch, it looks just like an acorn. It enjoys scaring other Pokémon.

Name: Nuzleaf
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if this Pokémon would be damaged by an attack, prevent that attack’s damage done to this Pokémon if that damage is 60 or less.
Flavor-Text: It lives deep in forests. With the leaf on its head, it makes a flute whose song makes listeners uneasy.

Name: Shiftry
Effects: Until the end of your opponent’s next turn, each Stadium or Pokémon Tool card in play has no effect. (This includes cards that come into play on that turn.)
If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: By flapping its leafy fan, it can whip up gusts of 100 ft/second that can level houses.

Name: Foongus
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench, you may put 3 Poké Ball cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: It lures Pokémon with its pattern that looks just like a Poké Ball then releases poison spores.

Name: Amoonguss
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is Confused, it is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It lures prey close by dancing and waving its arm caps, which resemble Poké Balls, in a swaying motion.

Name: Larvesta
Flavor-Text: The base of volcanoes is where they make their homes. They shoot fire from their five horns to repel attacking enemies.

Name: Volcarona
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused. If tails, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Discard all Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When volcanic ash darkened the atmosphere, it is said that Volcarona’s fire provided a replacement for the sun.

Name: Ponyta
Effects: Put 2 [R] Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: Its legs grow strong while it chases after its parent. It runs in fields and mountains all day.

Name: Rapidash
Effects: Discard a [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It gallops at nearly 150 mph. With its mane blazing ferociously, it races as if it were an arrow.

Name: Chimchar
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The gas made in its belly burns from its rear end. The fire burns weakly when it feels sick.

Name: Monferno
Flavor-Text: It uses ceilings and walls to launch aerial attacks. Its fiery tail is but one weapon.

Name: Infernape
Effects: Discard all [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
If you have fewer than 10 [R] Energy cards in your discard pile, this attack does nothing. Shuffle 10 [R] Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.
Flavor-Text: It tosses its enemies around with agility. It uses all its limbs to fight in its own unique style.

Name: Talonflame BREAK
Effects: Discard all [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
BREAK-Rule: Talonflame BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

Name: Litleo
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: They set off on their own from their pride and live by themselves to become stronger. These hot-blooded Pokémon are quick to fight.

Name: Pyroar
Effects: Search your deck for a [R] Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tool cards attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: With fiery breath of more than 10,000 degrees Fahrenheit, they viciously threaten any challenger. The females protect the pride’s cubs.

Name: Pyroar BREAK
Effects: This Pokémon does 50 damage to itself.
BREAK-Rule: Pyroar BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

Name: Volcanion
Effects: Choose 2 of your Benched Pokémon. Attach a [R] Energy card from your discard pile to each of those Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It expels its internal steam from the arms on its back. It has enough power to blow away a mountain.

Name: Volcanion-EX
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a [R] Energy card from your hand. If you do, during this turn, your Basic [R] Pokémon’s attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mantine
Effects: Discard as many cards as you like from your hand. Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon for each card you discarded in this way.
This attack does 90 damage minus 10 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: While elegantly swimming in the sea, it ignores Remoraid that cling to its fins seeking food scraps.

Name: Shellos
Flavor-Text: Its shape and coloration vary, depending on its habitat.

Name: Gastrodon
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon’s attacks cost [C] more, and its Retreat Cost is [C] more.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It apparently had a huge shell for protection in ancient times. It lives in shallow tidal pools.

Name: Oshawott
Flavor-Text: It fights using the scalchop on its stomach. In response to an attack, it retaliates immediately by slashing.

Name: Dewott
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: As a result of strict training, each Dewott learns different forms for using the scalchops.

Name: Samurott
Effects: If the damage from this attack reduces your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s HP to 60 or less, that Pokémon is Knocked Out.
This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: One swing of the sword incorporated in its armor can fell an opponent. A simple glare from one of them quiets everybody.

Name: Clauncher
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Through controlled expulsions of internal gas, it can expel water like a pistol shot. At close distances, it can shatter rock.

Name: Clawitzer
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a Special Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Mega Evolution Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: By expelling water from the nozzle in the back of its claw, it can move at a speed of 60 knots.

Name: Clawitzer BREAK
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn. During your next turn, any damage done to that Pokémon by attacks is increased by 120 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
BREAK-Rule: Clawitzer BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

Name: Bergmite
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Using air of -150 degrees Fahrenheit, they freeze opponents solid. They live in herds above the snow line on mountains.

Name: Avalugg
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Discard the top 3 cards of your deck. For each [W] Energy card you discarded in this way, discard the top 3 cards of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: The way several Bergmite huddle on its back make it look like an aircraft carrier made of ice.

Name: Mareep
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It stores lots of air in its soft fur, allowing it to stay cool in summer and warm in winter.

Name: Flaaffy
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: If its coat becomes fully charged with electricity, its tail lights up. It fires hair that zaps on impact.

Name: Ampharos
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put 3 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon-EX.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage. If tails, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: The tail’s tip shines brightly and can be seen from far away. It acts as a beacon for lost people.

Name: Joltik
Flavor-Text: They attach themselves to large-bodied Pokémon and absorb static electricity, which they store in an electric pouch.

Name: Galvantula
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. Apply Weakness and Resistance.
The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: When attacked, they create an electric barrier by spitting out many electrically charged threads.

Name: Nidoran Male
Effects: Search your deck for Nidoran Female and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: Its large ears are flapped like wings when it is listening to distant sounds. It extends toxic barbs when angered.

Name: Nidorino
Effects: If Nidorina is on your Bench, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: An aggressive Pokémon that is quick to attack. The horn on its head secretes a powerful venom.

Name: Nidoking
Ability-Effect: Your Nidoqueen’s attacks do 20 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Evolution Pokémon on your Bench.
Flavor-Text: One swing of its mighty tail can snap a telephone pole as if it were a matchstick.

Name: Drifloon
Effects: Move 1 damage counter from 1 of your Pokémon to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon are called the “Signpost for Wandering Spirits.” Children holding them sometimes vanish.

Name: Drifblim
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Discard 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon. Put 8 damage counters on your opponent’s Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: It’s drowsy in daytime, but flies off in the evening in big groups. No one knows where they go.

Name: Litwick
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and discard them. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: Litwick shines a light that absorbs the life energy of people and Pokémon, which becomes the fuel that it burns.

Name: Lampent
Effects: Each player either draws or discards cards until that player has 4 cards in his or her hand. (Your opponent does this first.)
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It arrives near the moment of death and steals spirit from the body.

Name: Chandelure
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may look at the top 2 cards of your deck and put 1 of them into your hand. Discard the other card.
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each Supporter card in your discard pile.
Flavor-Text: The spirits burned up in its ominous flame lose their way and wander this world forever.

Name: Hoopa
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
This Pokémon can’t use Portal Strike during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: It is said to be able to seize anything it desires with its six rings and six huge arms. With its power sealed, it is transformed into a much smaller form.

Name: Mankey
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Flop attack’s base damage is 50.
Flavor-Text: It lives in treetop colonies. If one becomes enraged, the whole colony rampages for no reason.

Name: Primeape
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
If, before doing damage, your opponent’s Active Pokémon has more remaining HP than this Pokémon, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It is always outrageously furious. If it gives chase, it will tenaciously track the target no matter how far.

Name: Nosepass
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Its magnetic nose consistently faces north. Travelers check Nosepass to get their bearings.

Name: Probopass
Effects: Your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: It freely controls three small units called Mini-Noses using magnetic force.

Name: Anorith
Ability-Effect: Each of your Restored Pokémon has no Weakness.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon ancestor that was reanimated from a fossil. It lived in the sea and hunted with claws.

Name: Armaldo
Effects: Move an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon to 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Protected by a hard shell, its body is very sturdy. It skewers prey with its claws to feed.

Name: Croagunk
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is Poisoned, put 3 more damage counters on that Pokémon between turns. This effect can be applied more than once.
Flavor-Text: Inflating its poison sacs, it fills the area with an odd sound and hits flinching opponents with a poison jab.

Name: Toxicroak
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s Poisoned Pokémon.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Its knuckle claws secrete a toxin so vile that even a scratch could prove fatal.

Name: Sneasel
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: Its paws conceal sharp claws. If attacked, it suddenly extends the claws and startles its enemy.

Name: Weavile
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may put a Pokémon Tool card attached to 1 of your Pokémon into your hand.
Flavor-Text: They live in cold regions, forming groups of four or five that hunt prey with impressive coordination.

Name: Spiritomb
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Move as many damage counters on your opponent’s Benched Pokémon as you like to any of your opponent’s other Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: It was bound to a fissure in an Odd Keystone as punishment for misdeeds 500 years ago.

Name: Pawniard
Effects: This attack does 10 damage times the number of your Pawniard.
Flavor-Text: Ignoring their injuries, groups attack by sinking the blades that cover their bodies into their prey.

Name: Bisharp
Effects: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent’s attack during his or her last turn, this attack does 60 more damage.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: Bisharp pursues prey in the company of a large group of Pawniard. Then Bisharp finishes off the prey.

Name: Yveltal
Effects: Attach a [D] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon can’t attack during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: When its life comes to an end, it absorbs the life energy of every living thing and turns into a cocoon once more.

Name: Yveltal BREAK
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
BREAK-Rule: Yveltal BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

Name: Steelix-EX
Effects: You may do 50 more damage. If you do, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 100 damage times the number of heads.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Steelix-EX
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Shieldon
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It was generated from a fossil dug out of a layer of clay that was older than anyone knows. It has a sturdy face.

Name: Bastiodon
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon equal to the damage done to this Pokémon.
This attack does 10 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.
Flavor-Text: Any frontal attack is repulsed. It is a docile Pokémon that feeds on grass and berries.

Name: Klink
Effects: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: The two minigears that mesh together are predetermined. Each will rebound from other minigears without meshing.

Name: Klang
Effects: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: A minigear and big gear comprise its body. If the minigear it launches at a foe doesn’t return, it will die.

Name: Klinklang
Ability-Effect: Any damage done to this Pokémon by an opponent’s attack is reduced by 10 for each [C] in your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Gear Spinner attack does 70 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Its red core functions as an energy tank. It fires the charged energy through its spikes into an area.

Name: Cobalion
Effects: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn. This Pokémon can’t use Quick Guard during your next turn.
This attack does 30 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.
Flavor-Text: It has a body and heart of steel. It worked with its allies to punish people when they hurt Pokémon.

Name: Magearna-EX
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of your opponent’s attacks, except damage, done to each of your Pokémon that has any [M] Energy attached to it. (Existing effects are not removed.)
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Soul Blaster attack’s base damage is 60.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Marill
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The tip of its tail is filled with oil that is lighter than water, so it acts as a float.

Name: Azumarill
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The bubble-like pattern on its stomach helps it camouflage itself when it’s in the water.

Name: Gardevoir-EX
Effects: If this Pokémon and your opponent’s Active Pokémon have the same amount of Energy attached to them, this attack does 70 more damage.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Gardevoir-EX
Effects: Discard as many of your Benched Pokémon as you like. This attack does 10 more damage for each Benched Pokémon you discarded in this way.
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Klefki
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may discard all cards attached to this Pokémon and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon as a Pokémon Tool card. Prevent any damage done to the Pokémon this card is attached to by attacks from your opponent’s Mega Evolution Pokémon. If this card is attached to a Pokémon, discard this card at the end of your opponent’s turn.
Flavor-Text: It never lets go of a key that it likes, so people give it the keys to vaults and safes as a way to prevent crime.

Name: Xerneas
Effects: Choose 2 of your Benched Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a [Y] Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Legends say it can share eternal life. It slept for a thousand years in the form of a tree before its revival.

Name: Xerneas BREAK
Effects: This attack does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
BREAK-Rule: Xerneas BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

Name: Druddigon
Effects: If your opponent has any Pokémon BREAK in play, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flip 2 coins. If both of them are tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It warms its body by absorbing sunlight with its wings. When its body temperature falls, it can no longer move.

Name: Deino
Effects: Your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Lacking sight, it’s unaware of its surroundings, so it bumps into things and eats anything that moves.

Name: Zweilous
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: After it has eaten up all the food in its territory, it moves to another area. Its two heads do not get along.

Name: Hydreigon
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 40 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Discard as many [D] Energy attached to your Pokémon as you like. This attack does 50 damage times the amount of [D] Energy you discarded in this way.
Flavor-Text: It responds to movement by attacking. This scary, three-headed Pokémon devours everything in its path!

Name: Hydreigon BREAK
Effects: Discard 3 Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
BREAK-Rule: Hydreigon BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

Name: Meowth
Effects: You can use this attack only if you go second, and only on your first turn. Discard an Energy attached to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Adores round objects. It wanders the streets on a nightly basis to look for dropped loose change.

Name: Persian
Effects: If this Pokémon evolved from Meowth during this turn, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Its lithe muscles allow it to walk without making a sound. It attacks in an instant.

Name: Aipom
Effects: Look at the top 3 cards of your opponent’s deck and put them back in any order.
Flavor-Text: Its tail is so powerful that it can use it to grab a tree branch and hold itself up in the air.

Name: Ambipom
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck. If that card is an Energy card, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: To eat, it deftly shucks nuts with its two tails. It rarely uses its arms now.

Name: Rufflet
Effects: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: They will challenge anything, even strong opponents, without fear. Their frequent fights help them become stronger.

Name: Braviary
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 more damage.
This attack does 120 damage minus 20 damage for each [C] in your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text: They fight for their friends without any thought about danger to themselves. One can carry a car while flying.

Name: Fletchling
Flavor-Text: Despite the beauty of its lilting voice, it’s merciless to intruders that enter its territory.

Name: Fletchinder
Flavor-Text: The hotter the flame sac on its belly, the faster it can fly, but it takes some time to get the fire going.

Name: Talonflame
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in your hand when you are setting up to play, you may put it face down as your Active Pokémon.
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: When attacking prey, it can reach speeds of up to 310 mph. It finishes its prey off with a colossal kick.

Name: Hawlucha
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench, you may have your opponent switch his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: With its wings, it controls its position in the air. It likes to attack from above, a maneuver that is difficult to defend against.

Name: Armor Fossil Shieldon
Description: Look at the bottom 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Shieldon you find there and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Captivating Poké Puff
Description: Your opponent reveals his or her hand. Put any number of Basic Pokémon you find there onto your opponent’s Bench.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Claw Fossil Anorith
Description: Look at the bottom 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal an Anorith you find there and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Gardevoir Spirit Link
Description: Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Gardevoir-EX.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Greedy Dice
Description: You can play this card only if you took it as a face-down Prize card, before you put it into your hand.Flip a coin. If heads, take 1 more Prize card.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Ninja Boy
Description: Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and switch it with that Pokémon. (Any attached cards, damage counters, Special Conditions, turns in play, and any other effects remain on the new Pokémon.) Shuffle the first Pokémon into your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Pokémon Ranger
Description: Remove all effects of attacks on each player and his or her Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Special Charge
Description: Shuffle 2 Special Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Steelix Spirit Link
Description: Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Steelix-EX.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Volcanion-EX
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a [R] Energy card from your hand. If you do, during this turn, your Basic [R] Pokémon’s attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: This Pokémon can’t attack during your next turn.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Steelix-EX
Effects: You may do 50 more damage. If you do, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.
Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 100 damage times the number of heads.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Steelix-EX
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Mega-Evolution-Rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Magearna-EX
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of your opponent’s attacks, except damage, done to each of your Pokémon that has any [M] Energy attached to it. (Existing effects are not removed.)
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Soul Blaster attack’s base damage is 60.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Gardevoir-EX
Effects: If this Pokémon and your opponent’s Active Pokémon have the same amount of Energy attached to them, this attack does 70 more damage.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: M Gardevoir-EX

Effects: This Pokémon can’t attack during your next turn.
